articles.description: Belligerence is back. Both the US and Iran are hitting each other and the statements from both sides do not auger well for a peace deal. The only positive is that the back channel talks are still apparently on, and no one is outrightly dismissing the possibility of a deal. The latest Gulf flare-up should normally be very bad news for the Indian markets. But the way they have been reacting bears a closer look.

Investors should select funds based on financial goals, investment horizon and risk appetite.

articles.title: AI robotics company Theker raises $85 million from investors including LVMH
articles.description: Theker, an AI robotics firm from Barcelona, has secured $85 million in new funding. This investment was led by CRV and included major players like Samsung and LVMH. Theker develops AI-powered robots for industrial tasks. This funding highlights the growing tech scene in Spain, following Amazon's significant investment in the country for data centers and AI.

articles.title: Explained: Why Oracle shares crashed 10% in extended trading despite earnings beat
articles.description: Oracle shares fell sharply in after-hours trading despite beating earnings estimates, as investors reacted to aggressive AI spending plans and rising debt concerns. The company’s massive capital raise and infrastructure push raised fears of over-leverage, even as strong revenue growth and bullish guidance highlighted its expanding role in AI cloud infrastructure.

articles.title: SC calls homemakers as 'nation builders', sets pay
articles.description: The Supreme Court of India has declared homemakers as nation builders. A monthly compensation of Rs. 30,000 will now be awarded for the loss of domestic and caregiving services. The court acknowledged the economic value of unpaid household work. This ruling recognizes the significant contributions of homemakers to families and society.

Electricity Minister C T R Nirmal Kumar reviewed measures to ensure continuous power for urban and suburban residents.

articles.title: ₹42-cr. fine slapped for stone quarry violation
articles.description: The Department of Mines has slapped a quarry operator with a hefty fine of ₹42 crore for quarrying 40,000 cubic metres of stones in excess of the permissible limit. Sources said the department officials conducted an inspection in a stone quarry at Singaththakurichi in Srivaikundam taluk a few months ago following complaints of excess mining of stones.
